issues: Missing handling for files added/removed directly on NAS (bypassing the app)
No details on resumable upload/download for large files
Vague conflict resolution for concurrent modifications to the same .material_index.json
No mention of how folders are represented in .material_index.json
Missing error handling for network/WebDAV server failures and file corruption
No strategy for handling partial updates or large index files
Unclear how to maintain consistency between Core Data (iOS) and Hive (Flutter)
No mention of timezone handling (need to confirm UTC-only for timestamps)
Missing specs for file name encoding (non-ASCII characters)
No plan for app uninstall/reinstall state recovery
suggestions: Add a section on detecting NAS-side file changes (e.g., compare file_modified_at with index entries)
Consider using WebDAV locks or optimistic concurrency control for index updates
Specify index pagination or incremental sync for large collections
Add authentication method options (e.g., API keys, OAuth2 if supported by NAS)
Define a clear folder representation schema in .material_index.json
Add specs for cache management and cleanup strategies
Consider a shared serialization/validation library for iOS and Flutter to ensure consistency
